H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78 (strong confidence). Strong right skew with 66% positive and peak at 8-9. Qualitative data (n=29) emphasizes noticeable improvement and high music liking; settling ~10 minutes. Highest YouTube views (30) in dataset. AutoGluon predicts 6.86 for overstimulation zone, indicating observed efficacy exceeds model expectations.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: People tend to feel significantly calmer after this session, especially when overwhelmed, and it's relatively well-discovered.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73 (strong confidence). 65% positive with central tendency at 7-8. Qualitative responses (n=21) show noticeable improvement and variable settling. YouTube views=5. AutoGluon prediction (6.86) closely matches observed mean.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Most users experience a good reduction in overstimulation, with effects appearing within a few minutes.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68 (meaningful confidence). 64% positive with distribution centered at 6-9. Qualitative feedback (n=23) indicates noticeable improvement and quick settling (5-10 minutes). YouTube views=8. AutoGluon predicts 6.86, with observed mean slightly above expectation.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Listeners generally feel better after this session, with calming effects setting in fairly quickly.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93 (strong confidence). Distribution shows positive skew with 63% scoring ≥6. Qualitative data (n=23) indicates most listeners report noticeable improvement within 5-10 minutes and positive music reception. YouTube reach is minimal (7 views). AutoGluon predicts 7.84 for anxiety zone, suggesting potential for optimization through increased exposure or minor protocol refinements.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Most listeners feel noticeably calmer after this anxiety session, with benefits appearing within ten minutes.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62 (meaningful confidence). Distribution clusters at 6-8 with 67% positive. Qualitative responses (n=20) show balanced improvement reports and quick settling (~5 minutes). YouTube views=6. AutoGluon predicts 7.84, indicating headroom for performance gains via enhanced engagement or parameter tuning.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: People generally report a mild to moderate improvement in anxiety symptoms, settling quickly.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89 (meaningful confidence). Even distribution across 5-9 range. Qualitative feedback (n=20) reveals mixed outcomes with some reporting no effect and slower settlement. YouTube views=4. AutoGluon prediction aligns at 7.84 but observed mean is lower, suggesting possible context-specific factors worth investigating.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Results are mixed but lean toward slight benefit, though some feel no change after listening.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70 (meaningful confidence). Lower mean aligns with crisis protocol design for acute states. Distribution shows concentration at 6-8 (53% positive). Qualitative data (n=24) highlights significant improvement for subset and rapid settling in some cases. YouTube views=6. AutoGluon predicts 7.96, indicating substantial gap warranting design investigation.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: This crisis protocol provides modest relief for many, with some experiencing significant improvement while others feel little change.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70 (meaningful confidence). 55% positive with spread across 5-8 range. Qualitative responses (n=16) note moderate to significant improvement and variable settling. YouTube views=4. AutoGluon predicts 7.96, underscoring underperformance relative to model expectations.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Listeners often note slight to moderate improvement in distress, but effects vary across individuals.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91 (meaningful confidence). 52% positive with wide distribution. Qualitative input (n=19) shows fastest settling among crisis protocols but mixed change ratings. YouTube views=5. AutoGluon maintains high prediction (7.96), suggesting mismatch between design and observed effects.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: While some users report quick relief, the overall impact feels inconsistent with frequent mixed responses.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86 (strong confidence). 65% positive with weight at 8-10. Qualitative data (n=15) shows majority noticing improvement and rapid settling (~5 minutes). YouTube views=4. AutoGluon does not provide zone-specific prediction but observed mean aligns with SLEEP series expectations.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Sleep-focused listeners commonly notice a clear improvement in restfulness, often within just a few minutes.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55 (meaningful confidence). 67% positive with tight distribution (6-9). Qualitative responses (n=15) indicate moderate improvement and occasional delayed settling (10-20 minutes). YouTube views=5. Consistent with high-reliability SLEEP profile.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Most people find this helpful for winding down before sleep, with consistent effects across listeners.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68 (meaningful confidence). 64% positive. Qualitative feedback (n=22) shows noticeable improvement and settling ~5-10 minutes. YouTube views=3. Performance remains stable across administrations.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Users typically feel a modest improvement in sleep quality, with benefits noticeable shortly after starting.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82 (meaningful confidence). 56% positive. Qualitative responses (n=23) emphasize noticeable improvement and quick settling. YouTube views=5. AutoGluon predicts 7.73 for FOCUS zone, indicating meaningful gap between observed and expected efficacy.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Focus improves modestly for many, with effects often appearing within the first five to ten minutes.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60 (meaningful confidence). 66% positive. Qualitative data (n=17) notes moderate to significant improvement and settling ~10 minutes. YouTube views=4. Performance aligns closely with AutoGluon prediction (7.73) within expected variance.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Users reliably report improved concentration and clarity, with effects settling in around ten minutes.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97 (meaningful confidence). 71% positive (highest in THRIVING). Qualitative responses (n=29) highlight strong improvement and balanced rhythm. YouTube views=15. AutoGluon predicts 7.73, with observed mean slightly below but within one standard deviation.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: This is the top performer for focus and clarity, with many users experiencing significant improvements.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73 (meaningful confidence). 53% positive. Qualitative data (n=21) reports slight to moderate improvement and frequent immediate settling. YouTube views=2. AutoGluon does not assign specific prediction but observed mean fits CREATIVE FLOW range.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Creative feel only slightly enhanced for most, with effects building gradually past the ten-minute mark.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41 (meaningful confidence). 58% positive. Qualitative responses (n=15) show significant improvement for subset and settling ~5-10 minutes. YouTube views=2. Consistent with moderate efficacy in CREATIVE FLOW.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: A modest boost in creative flow is commonly reported, with effects noticeable in about ten minutes.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32 (developing confidence). 67% positive with strong concentration at 7-9. Qualitative data (n=25) notes noticeable improvement and balanced rhythm reception. YouTube views=7. Represents highest observed mean in CREATIVE FLOW but requires larger n for confirmation.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: This version tends to give the strongest creative lift among its peers, with many users reporting clear benefits.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34 (meaningful confidence). 64% positive. Qualitative feedback (n=23) reports moderate improvement and variable settling (sometimes >20 minutes). YouTube views=3. AutoGluon predicts 7.69 for MAINTENANCE zone, suggesting modest underperformance.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Most people feel better able to maintain balance, though some take longer than twenty minutes to settle.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03 (meaningful confidence). 59% positive with wider spread. Qualitative responses (n=16) show mixed improvement and settling ~5-10 minutes. YouTube views=4. High variance warrants investigation into potential subgroup differences in response patterns.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Responses are mixed but lean toward slight improvement in balance, with effects appearing within ten minutes for many.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: n=14, mean=6.68, std=1.73 (developing confidence). 71% positive despite modest mean. Qualitative data (n=19) indicates noticeable improvement but frequent failure to settle. YouTube views=3. AutoGluon predicts 7.69, indicating meaningful gap between observed and model expectations.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: While many notice improvement in balance, a substantial number report failing to settle fully during the session.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94 (meaningful confidence). 54% positive. Qualitative responses (n=22) indicate moderate improvement and extended settling (~20 minutes). YouTube views=8. AutoGluon predicts 6.74 for MOOD&ENERGY zone, showing near-perfect alignment with observed mean.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Most listeners notice a gradual improvement in mood and energy, with effects building over about twenty minutes.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71 (meaningful confidence). 64% positive. Qualitative data (n=15) reports significant improvement and settling ~20 minutes. YouTube views=8. Performance matches AutoGluon prediction (6.74) within expected variance.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: People commonly report a clear lift in mood and energy, with effects typically appearing within twenty minutes.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74 (developing confidence). 56% positive. Qualitative feedback (n=25) shows noticeable improvement but frequent neutral/no effect reports. YouTube views=4. Slightly below AutoGluon prediction (6.74) suggesting potential for refinement.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Users often feel a slight improvement in mood and energy, though many report little to no change after listening.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01 (meaningful confidence). 45% positive (lowest in TRANSFORMING). Qualitative responses (n=17) show modest improvement and rapid settling. YouTube views=5. AutoGluon predicts 7.87 for MOTIVATION zone, revealing large disconnect between observed and expected efficacy.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Motivation effects are modest and inconsistent, with many users feeling little change despite rapid settling.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63 (developing confidence). 43% positive. Qualitative data (n=21) notes slight improvement and variable settling. YouTube views=7. Consistent with low-efficacy presentation in MOTIVATION series.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Users generally report minimal improvement in motivation, with effects being slight and hard to detect.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38 (developing confidence). 62% positive with high variance. Qualitative responses (n=17) report noticeable improvement despite spread. YouTube views=7. Represents high-outlier performance in MOTIVATION requiring larger sample for validation.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: This version shows the strongest motivation boost in the series, though individual responses vary widely.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62 (meaningful confidence). 54% positive. Qualitative responses (n=25) show noticeable improvement and strong music liking. YouTube views=8. AutoGluon predicts 7.54 for MINDCLEAR zone, suggesting observed mean falls short of model expectation.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Most users report clearer thinking after this session, with positive feelings about the music and rhythm.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64 (developing confidence). 56% positive. Qualitative data (n=20) indicates significant improvement for subset and settling ~10 minutes. YouTube views=12. Performance slightly below AutoGluon prediction (7.54) but within developmental range.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: People often experience improved mental clarity, with effects typically appearing within ten minutes.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45 (developing confidence). 50% positive with high spread suggesting bimodal response. Qualitative feedback (n=17) includes reports of negative effects and mixed change ratings. YouTube views=8. AutoGluon predicts 7.54, indicating substantial mismatch requiring design revision.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Results are highly mixed: some users love it while others feel no effect or even worse, suggesting it may not work for everyone.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The dataset shows consistent positive effects across protocols, with mean scores clustering near 7.0 and moderate variability.
• HEALING demonstrates the most consistent responses (lowest variance), aligning with its regulation focus.
• THRIVING shows slightly elevated mean scores, particularly in Hold protocols, suggesting optimization effects.
• TRANSFORMS exhibits greater spread, consistent with its ascending-arc design for lower-affect states.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Each service line displays patterns matching its intended physiological and psychological targets.
• HEALING protocols (especially DESCENT) show tight clustering around 7.0, indicating reliable nervous-system regulation.
• THRIVING Hold protocols (e.g., T-FC-L3) achieve the highest observed means, supporting cognitive enhancement goals.
• TRANSFORMING ASCENT protocols display wider distributions, reflecting work with low-arousal, exploratory states.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several protocols deviate from expected patterns within their series.
• X-MC-L3 (n=10, mean=5.7, std=2.45) shows unusually low scores for a Mind & Clarity DESCENT protocol.
• Crisis DESCENT protocols (H-CR-L1/2/3) consistently underperform relative to other HEALING entries.
• T-MB-L2 (n=22, std=2.03) exhibits high variance suggesting possible subgroup differences.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Targeted refinements could enhance efficacy and data quality.
• H-CR-L2 (n=33, mean=6.52) – test extended hold phase or adjusted frequency range to better support acute distress.
• X-MC-L3 (n=10, mean=5.7) – investigate potential bimodal response; consider L2 variant with modified descent slope.
• T-MB-L3 (n=14, mean=6.68) – increase duration or add entry cue to align with model-predicted outcomes.
• X-MD-L1 (n=22, mean=6.36) – develop L2 version with adjusted ascent curve to address underperformance against predictions.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding drift reveals evolving analytical interpretations, while clustering shows protocol relationships.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: 25 protocols show notable drift (similarity <0.85), especially T-MB-L1/L3 and T-CF-L3, indicating shifting response patterns.
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: T-MB-L2 and T-MB-L3 are most similar (0.844), while T-CF-L3 remains most distinct, suggesting unique feature interactions.
• Confidence: drift signals are based on limited version diversity; further runs will clarify analytical stability.
